name: Reduced Launch Cost 4.1
summary: Reduces the cost for launching your ship from the ground. Options for free cost, 5% and 10% of total energy per launch
author: Lex - lo2k
description:
[size=4]Reduced Launch Cost
[/size]
[b]4.1 version is compatible with Cursed 5.29[/b]

Removes or lowers the fuel cost for launching your ship from the ground.
[b]
[/b][b][color=#ff7700]Note 1 : Each ship type uses different fuel quantities when taking-off and playing in survival doubles fuel quantities !
[b]Note 2 : Since a few game updates, you can use supercharged slots to improve fuel efficiency, get ingame technology modules reducing fuel quantity used per launch or even slowly recharging your fuel tank when landed ![/b][/color][/b]

Options for free cost (instead of 25% of energy per launch), 10% and 5% of total energy per launch

[size=3]Installation[/size]
[list]
[*]Unzip the zip file
[*]Pick the desired pak
[list]
[*] 0% cost per launch: ReducedLaunchCost_0.pak
[*]5% cost per launch: Reduced Launch Cost 5.pak
[*]10% cost per launch: Reduced Launch Cost 10.pak
[/list][*]Copy the desired pak file to No Man's Sky/GAMEDATA/MODS (Create the folder if it doesn't exist)
[*]Delete No Man's Sky/GAMEDATA/PCBANKS/DISABLEMODS.txt if it exists
[/list]

[i]Note:[/i] If the mod doesn't seem to work, you likely have a mod conflict. Try renaming this mod to be last (e.g. XXReducedLaunchCost_0.pak)
[color=#f1c232]This mod is incompatible with any other mod that modifies[/color][b] [color=#f1c232]NMS_REALITY_GCTECHNOLOGYTABLE.MBIN[/color] [/b][color=#f1c232]but a .lua file is provided for each variant if you want to merge it with any other mod written for[/color][b] [/b][url=https://www.nexusmods.com/nomanssky/mods/957][color=#ffd966]AMUMSS[/color][/url][color=#f1c232]
[/color]

[size=3]Un-installation[/size]
[list]
[*]Delete the selected pak file from No Man's Sky/GAMEDATA/PCBANKS/MODS
[/list]

[size=3]Changes[/size]
File: METADATA/REALITY/TABLES/NMS_REALITY_GCTECHNOLOGYTABLE.MBIN

name: Rhesus_TOR's Police State Mod
summary: This mod causes the death of any planetary sentinel drone to immediately escalate your wanted level to 3.
author: Rhesus_TOR and re-uploaded by CyaNide1986
description:
Hi everyone, i'm here to present you another piece of work from an NMS modder named [b]Rhesus_TOR[/b].

[b]Rhesus_TOR[/b] was generous enough to give me the right to re-upload his mod(s) here on The Nexus and to be able to reach another huge and great batch of NMS mods users, as he prefer to concentrate his efforts on the NMSM site. I'm only a re-uploader here, all the credits for this mod goes to [b]Rhesus_TOR[/b], exept for the French translation of the mod description i made for the French NMS community. French users will find it below the following original English mod description. 

This is the second re-upload of a few anothers i will make here on The Nexus for you all. Hope you will like the mods as much as many others do.

Feel free to comment, post your own screenshots and feedback. I'll do my best to help on potential issues you encounter and report them to [b]Rhesus_TOR[/b], if he don't answer them himself.

As stated above, French translation will follow original English mod description.


Mod description :

This mod causes the death of any planetary sentinel drone to immediately escalate your wanted level to 3. Additionally, this mod removes the warning timer so killing the first planetary sentinel drone will cause additional waves of sentinels to spawn immediately. Once you've defeated the first sentinel walker, from that point onwards (until you die or find a means of escape), elite walker sentinel groups with quads and drones will spawn endlessly.Once attacked, these sentinels don't hesitate, are faster than your typical drones, are more likely to catch you, and will open fire immediately. 

WARNING ! This mod can get you killed quickly.  While it's true you can farm sentinels indefinitely with this mod, you also stand a good chance of getting your ass handed to you.  Be prepared to reload from a previous save. 

NOTE: This mod IS compatible with [b]Decky's[/b] [url=http://www.nexusmods.com/nomanssky/mods/129/?]Walkers[/url] mod, but seriously, combining the two mods can get you eaten alive.
[i]

[/i]Mod installation instructions :

[i]Open the .ZIP file and copy the .PAK file into your PCBANKS folder.  Enjoy!


[/i]Changelog :

1.0, 2016-10-06 7:12am US-ET

▪ Made assorted changes to GCROBOTGLOBALS.MBIN, reducing drone tolerance times once attacked to ZERO.

▪ Changed WantedLevel to 3 in the Destructible portion of the file, MODELS  - COMMON - ROBOTS - DRONE - ENTITIES - DRONE.ENTITY.MBIN.

▪ Changed multiple values in the file, METADATA - SIMULATION - ECOSYSTEM - ROLEDESCRIPTIONTABLES - SENTINELS - GROUNDTABLESENTINELS.MBIN in order to make Quad and Walker spawns more numerous IF (and only IF) Walkers or Quads are enabled via creature table updates (see Decky's [url=http://www.nexusmods.com/nomanssky/mods/129/?]Walkers[/url] mod for more information).



[b]All credits[/b] must go to [b]Rhesus_TOR[/b], the original mod author. Thanks so much to him for giving me the right to re-upload his mod(s) on The Nexus site. If you like this mod, please endorse it !

Other Rhesus_TOR's Mods available :

- [url=http://www.nexusmods.com/nomanssky/mods/295/?]Rhesus_TOR's Steady Cam Mod[/url]



French translation made by CyaNide1986/ Traduction francaise par CyaNide1986 :


Salut tout le monde. Je suis ici pour vous présenter un autre projet d'un moddeur de NMS, le dénommé Rhesus_TOR.

Rhesus_TOR fut assez généreux pour m'autoriser à re-uploader ses mods sur le Nexus et ainsi permettre à une énorme et géniale communauté de profiter de son travail, vu qu'il préfère pour l'instant  concentrer ses efforts sur le site NMSM.

Je ne suis qu'un simple re-uploader, [b]tout le mérite revient à Rhesus_TOR,[/b] mis a part la traduction française que j'ai fait à l'attention de la communauté française de NMS. Ceci n'est que le deuxième re-upload sur le Nexus d'une longue liste. J'ose espérer que vous apprécierez ces mods autant que les autres les apprécient.

Soyez a l'aise de commenter, de publier vos screenshots et impressions. Je ferais mon possible pour vous aidez si jamais vous rencontrez des problemes et j'en informerai Rhesus_TOR si il ne vous a pas deja repondu.


Description du mod :

Ce mod élimine d'un coup toutes les sentinelles des planètes afin de monter votre niveau de recherche à 3. De plus, il enlève le délai d'avertissement, donc tuer la première sentinelle d'une planète causera immédiatement l'apparition des vagues additionnelles de sentinelles. Une fois que vous aurez tuer votre premier Walker sentinelle, à partir de ce moment ( jusqu'à ce que vous mourrez ou trouvez un moyen de vous échapper ), des groupes d'élite de walker sentinelles avec des quads et des drones apparaîtront sans fin. Une fois attaquées, ces sentinelles n'hésiteront plus à vous poursuivre,  elles sont plus rapides que les sentinelles normales et ouvrirons le feu immédiatement.[i]

[/i]ATTENTION !  S'il est avéré que vous pouvez farmer les sentinelles indéfiniment grâce à ce mod, il est également capable de ruiner votre partie. Soyez préparez à recharger votre save.

NOTE: Ce mod EST compatible avec le mod [url=http://www.nexusmods.com/nomanssky/mods/129/?]Walkers[/url] de [b]DeckyDoodles. [/b]Il EST compatible, mais sérieusement, combinez ces deux mods et vous allez en ch**r ![i]

[/i]
Instructions d'installation du mod :

[i]Ouvrez le fichier .zip et copiez le .pak dans le dossier PCBANKS. Amusez-vous !


[/i]Changelog :

1.0, 2016-10-06 7:12am US-ET

▪ Changements apportés à GCROBOTGLOBALS.MBIN, réduction des tolérances des drones à zéro.

▪ Changement de la valeur WantedLevel à 3 dans la partie Destructible du fichier, MODELS  - COMMON - ROBOTS - DRONE - ENTITIES - DRONE.ENTITY.MBIN.

▪ Changements multiples des valeurs dans le fichier, METADATA - SIMULATION - ECOSYSTEM - ROLEDESCRIPTIONTABLES - SENTINELS - GROUNDTABLESENTINELS.MBIN dans le but de permettre aux Quad et aux Walker d'apparaître en plus grands nombres si ( et seulement si ) les Walkers ou les Quads sont activés via des updates pour la valeur de *creature table*  ( voir le mod [url=http://www.nexusmods.com/nomanssky/mods/129/?]Walkers[/url] de DeckyDoodles pour plus d'informations ).



Tout le mérite doit revenir à [b]Rhesus_TOR[/b] , l'auteur original de ce Mod[b]. [/b]Un grand merci a lui de m'avoir permis de re-uploader son(ses) mod(s) sur le Nexus. Si vous aimez ce mod, s'il vous plaît endorsez-le !


Autres mods par Rhesus_TOR disponibles :

name: Unlimited Tech Stacking
summary: 9999 tech stacking.
author: Discount Dimebag
description:
Have you ever been annoyed by the maximum amount of tech being limited to 3 before they overload? 


Well now it's 9999.



Installation:
Unzip the file and copy the .pak to No Man's Sky\GAMEDATA\PCBANKS\MODS


Enjoy being OP.

Changelog:

Version 2, now with different amounts of stacking, 2x, 4x, and 6x. As well as the normal 9999.

I modified the GCGAMEPLAYGLOBALS.GLOBAL.MBIN file, so this will likely conflict with any mod that does that as well.

Only mod i know it conflicts with right now is Starship out of range and Faster scanners.

name: Project Atlas-Procedural Planet Colors Overhaul for Synthesis
summary: This mod adds procedurally generated Color pallete tables for every planetary elements, Creating visual color diversity on a scale never before possible. This mod is generated procedurally for No Man's Sky Synthesis and a product of my Project Atlas Universe Generator.
author: thegamer3006 or moddingaccount
description:
[b] [/b]

[center]PROJECT ATLAS UNIVERSE GENERATOR: PROCEDURAL PLANET COLORS MOD

--------------------------FULLY PROCEDURALLY GENERATED MOD. NOTHING MADE BY HAND--------------------------[/center]

[b]ABOUT:  This is a procedurally generated mod, part of my Project Atlas universe generator, that I designed for No Man’s Sky. This mod focuses on adding procedurally generated planet color palettes to all aspects of planets in No Man’s Sky. This mod is part of my Project Atlas Universe Generator for No Man's Sky. To know what Project Atlas is , check this reddit post that contains details about it.
[/b]
https://www.reddit.com/r/NoMansSkyTheGame/comments/96gyi4/introducing_project_atlas_your_very_own/


[b][center]----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------[/center][/b]
[b]GAME VERSION SUPPORTED: The mod works with No Man’s Sky Synthesis update and above.
[/b]
[b][center]----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------[/center][/b]
[b]SOME FEATURES OF THE MOD:  
[/b]
These are some of the salient features of the mod.

• Over half a million unique, procedurally generated colors in each version.
• 4 different versions.
• The color palletes give rise to billions of color combinations per planet type, resulting in immense global color variety.
• Colors are specifically themed to each planet type, resulting in visually appealing planetary color features.
• Immense local color variety per planet in addition to the already mentioned global color variety, with nearly 12 or more varied colors maximum possible for all elements visible at a time on any given planet.
• Uniquely color themed planets are possible with the mod.
• Night time looks magical with certain color palletes, creating a great experience.
• For some planets, all the elements have their colors picked in such a way that they are complements of other elements.
• Truly alien looking planet colors are possible.
• Beautifully colored planet rings, that give rise to glowing, pleasant and vibrant rings, not possible in vanilla game.
• Beautifully colored sea shores are possible.
• Desert planets now have a chance of getting planets that appear fully colored in sand.
• Snow planets have a extended range of possible snow colors.
• Snow covered peaks are now possible.
• Extended zones of multicolor grass on lush planets, that can stretch far and wide.
• Colors for each Fauna and flora also have lots of variation per animal, with the various species on a planet having more varied color combinations than on vanilla game.
• The more you explore, the greater the chance of finding more unique color combination, giving an incentive to actually explore.
• The different terrain features have better coloring, with mountain, terrain snakes, blobs etc having better,varied colors instead of having the same planet color applied to them.
• The buildings on a planet are also procedurally colored, with a more expanded color variety.


[b][center]----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------[/center]

[/b]
[b]VERSIONS OF THE MOD: 
[/b]
The mod has 4 versions, each version adds more than half a million unique, procedurally generated color palettes to the game that affects all aspects of a planet like terrain, flora, fauna, rocks, creatures, buildings,planet rings etc..these color palletes give rise to millions of color combinations resulting in a great amount of variety.

1) [b]_PROJECT_ATLAS_LUSH_COLORS_NORMAL.pak [/b]– This version adds procedural color palletes to only Lush type biomes. It contains more visually appealing color generation,that are themed to lush planets specifically, that look good both in the game and in screenshots. Less chance of alienish planets but more chance of visually appealing planets in terms of colors.

2) [b]_PROJECT_ATLAS_LUSH_COLORS_ENHANCED.pak[/b] – This version adds procedural color palletes to only Lush type biomes. It has features of the normal version, plus it allows for more alien and sci-fi color combinations , with color generation having much more variety. Gives more alien,varied,vibrant and unique looking planets in terms of colors.

3) [b]_PROJECT_ATLAS_PLANET_COLORS_NORMAL.pak[/b] - This version adds procedural color palletes to all biomes of the game, except weird biomes. It contains more visually appealing color generation,that are themed to each biome type specifically, that look good both in the game and in screenshots. Less chance of alienish planets but more chance of visually appealing planets in terms of colors, that fit the respective planet type.

4) [b]_PROJECT_ATLAS_PLANET_COLORS_ENHANCED.pak[/b] – This version adds procedural color palletes to all biomes of the game, except weird biomes. It has features of the normal version, plus it allows for more alien and sci-fi color combinations , with color generation having much more variety. Gives more alien,varied,vibrant and unique looking planets in terms of colors.

[b]Choose only one of the mod versions and place them in the MODS folder to make the mod work.

[/b]
[b][center]----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------[/center]

[/b]
[b]COMPATIBILITY: 
[/b]
The mod alters the following files and as such won’t work with any other mod that alters the same file: 

[b]GCTERRAINGLOBALS.GLOBAL.MBIN[/b]
[b]METADATA\SIMULATION\SOLARSYSTEM\BIOMES\BIOMEFILENAMES.MBIN

[/b]
[b][center]----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------[/center]

[/b]
[b]INSTALL INSTRUCTIONS:
[/b]
[b]1) Select only one of the four mod versions[/b]
2) Create a MODS folder in the GAMEDATA/PCBANKS/ folder of your No Man’s Sky install directory. Make sure to delete the DISABLEMODS.txt in the PCBANKS folder to make mods work.
3) Place the selected pak file in the Above MODS folder and play.


[b][center]----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------[/center]

[/b]
[b]BUGS AND SUGGESTIONS: 
[/b]
[b]Known bugs: [/b]
• This mod has a good chance of affecting the colors of your existing planet. Hence have a backup of the save before using the mod.
• When flying into a planet, there is a chance that the color of the planet in space might not be exactly the same as the one you get once you land on the planet.


If any other bugs are encountered, leave a comment on the mod page and I will get to it when I have time.
Any suggestions/feedback, leave them on the comment of the mod page, I will try and incorporate it, if its possible and if I have time.

[b][center]----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------[/center]
[/b]
[b]CREDITS:
[/b]
@EXOSOLAR  - for helping with many important aspects of the mod.
@MONKEYMAN192 – for keeping mbin compiler up and running.
 @codenameawesome for figuring out how to prevent the mod from changing ship colors . This was a major issue and he helped fix it and helped with some more stuff too.
 @BOSS KONG for helping test the mod and offering valuable input to the mod.
 @REDMAS for helping with some stuff in the mods.
a few more people who helped with few issues of the mod.

[b][center]----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------[/center][/b]

[b]---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------[/b]

files.changelog_html:
v1.2 changelog:

1)vastly improved procedural color assignment algorithms and added several new color assignment methods.
2) packed several times more colors in a single table, thereby reducing the need of color tables from 3000 to 1500 without reducing variety
3) fixed a myriad of crashes.
4)Ships wont change colors with the mod anymore!!!!!!!!!!!!

The mod has been tested by several testers for nearly 5 days and has been found to be working without any issues on both Legacy and NEXT saves.

name: Better Planet Generation
summary: A huge planet generation overhaul that aims at making your hundreds of hours of exploration in the magnificent, infinite universe of No Man's Sky less repetitive and hopefully even more memorable.
author: WoodyMontana
description:
[size=5][size=3][size=4][size=3][size=5][i][b]                                                     [size=6]Better Planet Generation[/size][/b][/i][/size]

[img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/1634/images/797/797-1629738968-1312445652.jpeg[/img]

[size=4][b]More planets

[/b][size=3]Better Planet Generation is a total overhaul of the planetary generation in No Man's Sky.[/size] [size=3]It improves by a significant margin the procedural aspect of the universe while at the same time respecting the original work and vision from Hello Games.[/size]

[size=3]It adds thousands of new distinct environments to the universe[/size][/size]. All of them come in five different life levels and varying placement scales and layouts. These environments were made to seamlessly combine with each other in many different ways.

Better Planet Generation is designed to bring variety to the different biomes you will visit but most of all it brings a lot of variation to the universe. Outside of the countless combinations of assets and their resulting new environments, planets you visit will present themselves differently each time. Placement layouts, scale of the regions, density, variety of assets and their respective scale, ...

No planet will look exactly the same. Unless, of course, it is a very simple one. More on that below.

[img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/1634/images/797/797-1631005159-472402214.jpeg[/img][img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/1634/images/797/797-1630936034-1789086190.jpeg[/img][img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/1634/images/797/797-1610300150-1298994048.jpeg[/img]


[size=5][size=3][size=4][size=3][size=4][b]Sub Biomes[/b][/size][/size][/size][/size][/size]

Caves and underwater sub biomes have also been entirely overhauled.

Tons of new variants have been added underground and just like on the surface, additional objects can spawn to increase variety even more.

﻿[img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/1634/images/797/797-1630410276-924973523.jpeg[/img]﻿

Underwater has been completely revamped and is now generated through a triple layered dynamic framework combining assets to generate highly varied and detailed environments.

[img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/1634/images/797/797-1629738939-165524032.jpeg[/img]
[b][size=4]
Unique planets

[/size][/b][size=4][size=3]On top of all the added variety, BPG also uses a flexible dynamic generation framework that can take many shapes[/size][/size] and create countless planetary variations, even when the same environments are being used multiple times.

Each planet generated has its own selection of parts based on its biome. Some liberties have been taken when designing and attributing those parts to the various biomes. Some objects have been injected to improve variety, but some restrictions have also been applied to preserve each biome's identity. Better Planet Generation also introduces countless restored, modified or repurposed assets to the game in order to increase the variety while preserving its visual style.

[img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/1634/images/797/797-1630936068-132859967.jpeg[/img][img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/1634/images/797/797-1630936034-754906087.jpeg[/img]

[/size][/size][/size][/size]
[size=5][size=3][size=4][size=3][size=5][size=3][size=4][size=3][b][size=4]Natural Generation[/size][/b][/size][/size][/size][/size]

Better Planet Generation is a vastly different experience compared to the unmodded game and even other biome mods.
Not even taking into account the small details, it is more than likely that you will never be able to encounter all the possible assets combinations this mod can generate. Let alone in the different regional layouts each dynamic template can result in.

Each biome has access to multiple procedural templates. Each producing different results in terms of layouts and objects combinations.
Their common goal is to show you a different kind of environment each time. Aesthetically and structurally.
No more "postcard" planets where most of what there is to see is right around the corner. The scale of the generation can get extremely vast compared to the vanilla game.

Planetary generation can now affect your visual enjoyment and the gameplay.
It is a video game, after all.

A world generated at its fullest possible level and highest complexity can take you some time to fully explore on foot. Meaning seeing the most notable regions (and not all of them) and scanning everything.

[img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/1634/images/797/797-1630052272-1155900954.jpeg[/img][img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/1634/images/797/797-1610300150-2086924238.jpeg[/img][img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/1634/images/797/797-1630272224-118529207.jpeg[/img]
[size=5][size=3][size=4][size=3][size=5][size=3][size=4][size=3][b][size=4]More Variation[/size][/b][/size][/size][/size][/size][/size][/size][/size][/size]

On the other hand, Better Planet Generation also allows more simple, generic yet still visually interesting planets to see :

- Planets only covered in grass and some plants, rocks or both.
- More barren worlds dominated by giant stone or crystal spires.
- Radioactive wastelands.
- Planets dotted with trees or occasional small forests.
- Volcanic wastelands.
- Toxic fields.
- Rocky swamps.
- ...

And the list goes on and on.
[size=5][size=3][size=4][size=3]The occurence of these "generic" planets only relies on probabilites. None of my templates is designed to specifically produce them.
I made sure they still looked good, though.[/size][/size][/size][/size]

[img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/1634/images/797/797-1630410296-1590906166.jpeg[/img][img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/1634/images/797/797-1626079315-1486130072.jpeg[/img]

[size=5][size=3][size=4][size=3][size=5][size=3][size=4][size=3][size=5][size=3][size=4][size=3][b][size=4]Different Experience[/size][/b][/size][/size][/size][/size][/size][/size][/size][/size][/size][/size][/size][/size]

Better Planet Generation is not for everyone. Planets can be both very detailed in every aspect or very simple.
If you expect to always see trees and a full grass coverage on your Lush planet or anywhere else, there are other mods for that.
This is not what you should expect using BPG.

Now, whether you are a new player ready to explore an infinite universe or a more weathered one that wants to be surprised by the game again, I suggest you give Better Planet Generation a try. It won't harm your game in any way.

[img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/1634/images/797/797-1629923717-282604290.jpeg[/img][img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/1634/images/797/797-1630410307-2137550589.jpeg[/img]

[size=5][size=3][size=4][size=3][b][size=5]WARNING BEFORE INSTALLING :[/size][/b]

Installing this mod will operate changes to the terrain in every biomes.
It is also recommended to install it while in a space station / anomaly / freighter and not on a planet's surface.
Some major updates to the mod can reset the terrain or biome lists.
I'll include a warning on the download page and the sticky when it's the case.[/size][/size][/size][/size]


Recommended :
[/size][/size]
[url=https://www.nexusmods.com/nomanssky/mods/968]DUD's Sky - Colors[/url] : load planetary colour palettes before BPG to avoid procedural textures conflicts.
[/size][/size]

files.name: Better Planet Generation 1.0
files.description: First release

files.name: Better Planet Generation 1.1
files.description: Version 1.1: Removed all restriction to biome objects.

files.name: Better Planet Generation 1.2
files.description: Tweaked object placement even further.

files.name: Better Planet Generation 1.3
files.description: Improved placement again. Reduced overall density to reduce overlaping of assets and increase performance.

files.name: Better Planet Generation 1.4
files.description: Added a third table in the mix. Adjusted overall density and scale in consequence. Generation scale is way larger and variety is increased significantly.

files.name: Better Planet Generation 1.5
files.description:
Redid the mod from scratch :
- restored grass and huge forest
- the third table is now a biome-themed megaliths table
- more tables added to the mix
- all biomes affected, except dead and weird biomes

files.name: Better Planet Generation 1.6
files.description: Improved placement, especially for megaliths. Might have to tone them down later.

files.name: Better Planet Generation 1.7
files.description: Finished the main framework of the mod and improved generation, placement and variety.

files.name: Better Planet Generation 1.8
files.description:
Version 1.8 :
-Significantly improved level of detail. Especially on the ground
-New underwater tables
-New cave tables
-New mountain tables
-Custom underwater props tables on the surface

files.name: Better Planet Generation 1.8a
files.description: I apparently packed some wrong files in last upload. They were unfinished and could cause all sorts of issues.

files.name: Better Planet Generation 2.0
files.description:
- Removed major restrictions from object tables selection
- Significantly improved diversity between planets
- Added new tables
- Balanced existing tables
- Added new custom placement rules
- Reduced the probability to find planets mostly devoid of grass

files.name: Better Planet Generation 2.1
files.description:
-Made megaliths spawn more frequently
-Reduced the object bloat on planets by reducing maximum number of object tables
-Had a negative impact on variety
-Fixed instances of crashes
-Full planets are still possible
-Barren planets are more frequent

files.name: Better Planet generation 2.5
files.description: Latest version. See sticky for details.

files.name: Better Planet generation 2.6
files.description:
- Tweaks and fixes
- Improved Megaliths
- More varied dead planets

files.name: Better Planet Generation II
files.description:
Newest version of the mod. Rebuilt from scratch to allow more variety.
Still in beta. Needs some testing and polishing, but it's functional.
Feedback welcome.

files.name: Better Planet Generation II
files.description: See sticky post for update highlights.

files.name: Better Planet Generation II
files.description: Re-upload that fixes an issue causing some toxic planets to be empty.

files.name: Better Planet Generation
files.description:
Final version.
Go to a freighter/space station before upgrading.
Lots of tweaks and additions. Streamlining of planet types. Removal of redundant templates and refinement of remaining ones.
Last update before Beyond.

files.name: Better Planet Generation
files.description: First Version For Beyond.

files.name: Better Planet Ecosystem
files.description: Makes the ecosystem more random, with a chance for large creatures or large packs.

files.name: Better Visuals
files.description:
Improves lighting, skies, fog and water visuals.
Also make objects color palettes more coherent.
Works best with the optional vibrant filter MOD.
No Fade (Terrain+Objects) is included in the MOD.

files.name: Vibrant Filter
files.description:
Makes the vibrant filter always active.

files.description:
More surprising ecosystem with tougher creatures, especially the bigger ones.
Predators spot you from farther away and passive creatures defend themselves more fiercely.

Better contrast with sharper visuals.
Automatic matching of color palettes (compatible with color palettes MODs)

files.description:

CAUTION : Modifies terrain generation.

Removed the terrain unlocker as I have found that despite changing the terrain dramatically, it actually reduced the overall variety.

files.description:

Same voxel settings but also includes the terrain unlocking from versions prior to 3.0.

Adds some handcrafted planet types the the game, thus increasing the pool of available "parts" that will be used to create procedurally generated planets.

Overall, a far more varied and coherent version of the MOD.

Uses the same Voxel settings as the regular Terrain Module.

name: Ger's Filmic ReShade v1.2
summary: A ReShade preset, giving the game a more filmic feel, rather than the stock stylised toon-like feel.
author: Geraint Thomas
description:
[color=#a2c4c9][size=5][b]Ger's Filmic ReShade v1.2
[size=4](updated for Exo Mech)[/size][/b][/size][/color]

The aim of this ReShade was to lean the game away from its stylised and toon-like colours, and more towards a next-gen looking one. It's a lightweight ReShade with only a handful of shaders active, but they work together harmoniously. By trade, I'm a Lecturer in Visual Effects for Film, which spurred me onto creating a filmic feel for this game.

The changes/shaders are as follows:

[list]
[*][b][color=#b4a7d6]Levels[/color][/b] - The main focus of this was to better hide the darker sides of planets, so that they blend in with space.
[*][color=#b4a7d6][b]Guassian Blur[/b][/color][color=#666666][color=#c27ba0] [/color][color=#ffffff]- The aim of this was to help turn the perfect world/geometry of a computer into the imperfect world of a camera lens. Softening the image, even slightly, helps give this a film feel as your eyes aren't being assaulted by pixel perfect lines.[/color][/color]
[*][color=#b4a7d6][b]Colourfulness[/b][/color] - Or lack of. This shader actually desaturates the image overall to bring the image to a more neutral look, and to compensate for the levels boosting chroma.
[*][color=#b4a7d6][b]Film Grain[/b][/color] - Self explanatory. Film grain takes the scene away from perfectly generated imagery and more towards a camera feel.
[*][color=#b4a7d6][b]Eye Adaption[/b][/color] - To help with the following bloom effect, this darkens the image should what your looking at appear too bright.
[*][color=#b4a7d6][b]Magic Bloom[/b][/color] - This has been specifically tweaked to bloom everything within the image, but the way it works is much more towards that of an ambient light (with more control). This helps bleed skies into grounds, gives planets an atmosphere, creates atmospheric lighting within brighter situations, creates realistic light wrapping when a dark subject is in front of a brighter one, and on.
[/list]
The result is an image that is much more suited towards a filmic, even next-gen game approach.

I'll let the screenshots speak for themselves.
[b](Installation is below screenshots)[/b]

[img]https://i.imgur.com/yWMgANJl.jpg[/img]
[img]https://i.imgur.com/bVJoowVl.jpg[/img]
[img]https://i.imgur.com/Wg2JVyPl.jpg[/img]
[img]https://i.imgur.com/tSzRK5nl.jpg[/img]
[img]https://i.imgur.com/4SnnDoll.jpg[/img]
[img]https://i.imgur.com/VyRdyitl.jpg[/img]
[img]https://i.imgur.com/FgZhj2cl.jpg[/img]
[img]https://i.imgur.com/1z6o2mNl.jpg[/img]
[img]https://i.imgur.com/Cd1We4Nl.jpg[/img]
[img]https://i.imgur.com/tjersmUl.jpg[/img]
[img]https://i.imgur.com/vyApYxPl.jpg[/img]
[img]https://i.imgur.com/PacrG9el.jpg[/img]
[img]https://i.imgur.com/FUL1cRpl.jpg[/img]
[img]https://i.imgur.com/njnkrFLl.jpg[/img]
[img]https://i.imgur.com/0BxA9EFl.jpg[/img]
[img]https://i.imgur.com/zDp7QTul.jpg[/img]

[b][color=#a2c4c9][size=5]Installation.[/size][/color][/b]

[list=1]
[*]Download ReShade (reshade.me).
[*]Launch the setup, choose No Man's Sky from the list, then chose [b]'Use Selected Application'.[/b]
[*]On the first 'Select Effect Packages to Install' window, [b]uncheck all [/b](leaving Standard Effects checked) and click next.
[*]On the next 'Select "Standard effects" files to install" window, [b]uncheck all[/b] and click next.
[*]Click on the [b]'Enable/disable ReShade in Vulkan globally'[/b] then [b]check the 'Enable' box[/b], otherwise you'll have to open this window each time you launch the game. Don't worry, no effects will be installed into your other Vulkan games as they'd have to have shader files in the install folder. This merely allows the ReShade capability into other games.
[*]Head into your NMS 'binaries' folder, then extract the contents of the zip file here. You should have a 'reshade-shaders' folder, and another file named 'Filmic ReShade.ini'. Overwrite any files.
[*]Launch the game.
[/list]
If the effect doesn't load, wait until you get to your load screen, and press the Home key on your keyboard. Select the 'Filmic ReShade' preset from the drop down menu at the top (you may have to click Reload once you do).


[color=#a2c4c9][size=5][b]Notes.

[/b][/size][/color][list]
[*]If you've installed the game to a different directory other than 'C:\Program Files (x86)\Steam', then open the 'opengl32.ini' file and amend the four directories.
[*]This does have a noteable FPS hit, so ensure your computer is up to it! Failing this, look at numerous NMS performance guides online.
[*]I strongly recommend using the Black Space with Nebulae mod, as seen in the screenshots, found here on Nexus. This allows the planets to glow against black, and for the shaded sides to blend in with space.
[/list]

Happy trails!

name: AMUMSS (auto modbuilder-updater with MOD script definition system)
summary: Revolutionary No Man's Sky AMUMSS (auto modbuilder/updater with MOD script definition system)Fully automatic mod builder that automates every step of nms modding and provides an easy to use lua script mod definition system.
author: Mjjstral aka MetaIdea and Wbertro aka TheBossBoy
description:
[color=#ff0000][size=6]-> [/size][size=5]Looking for lua scripts, see:[/size][size=6] [url=https://github.com/MetaIdea/nms-amumss-lua-mod-script-collection]AMUMSS LUA MOD SCRIPT ARCHIVE[/url] <-[/size][/color]

[font=Verdana][color=#ffe599][size=6]AMUMSS is NOT a mod manager, nor can it be used as such.  It is a script processor.[/size][/color][/font]

[color=#ff0000][size=5]AMUMSS does NOT need updating to continue working when NMS updates.[/size]
[size=4]       When the tool MBINCompiler.exe is updated, AMUMSS will download it automatically.[/size][/color]
AMUMSS will auto-update itself (when allowed), if a new version is available (Just execute BUILDMOD.bat as usual and read simple instructions)
[list]
[*]The tools MBINCompiler/libMBIN are auto-updated by AMUMSS.[/*]
[*]Scripts are NEVER updated by AMUMSS, you are responsible for using updated scripts, when they need to be.[/*]
[/list]
[b][b][color=#ff7700][size=3]Questions will be answered if asked in NMS Discord[/size]: here[/color] [url=https://discord.gg/22ZAU9H][color=#00ff00]https://discord.gg/22ZAU9H[/color]
[/url][/b]
IMPORTANT NOTES:[/b]
[list]
[*][b]This tool works as long as MBINCompiler can compile/decompile the MBIN touched by the scripts[/b][/*]
[*][b]NEW version [color=#ff00ff]does[/color] requires [i][color=#ffff00].NET 6 Desktop Runtime x64[/color][/i] to be installed (a MBINCompiler.exe requirement)  It will attempt to auto-install.[/b][/*]
[*][b][color=#ff0000]Note:[/color] Sometimes, your anti-virus may detect some component of AMUMSS and block/quarantine it.  Be assure it is not a virus but its [i][color=#ff0000]behavior [/color][/i]may be interpreted as such by some anti-virus.  Please make sure to create an exception in your anti-virus when this happens.  Also a reboot may be required as some anti-virus do not correctly register the exception when created.[/b][/*]
[/list]
[b][i][size=3]INCLUDES:[/size][/i][/b]
[list]
[*][b][i][size=3][b][i][size=3]NMSPE [/size][/i][/b]([u][b]NMS PCBANKS Explorer)[/b][/u] a [color=#ff0000][b]GUI app to explore libMBIN.dll, NMS PAKS and Unpack/Decompile/Import-Search EXML content[/b][/color][/size][/i][/b][/*]
[*][b][i][size=3][b][i][size=3]AMM [/size][/i][/b]([u][b]_ModScript_Manager)[/b][/u] a [color=#ff0000][b]GUI app simplifying usage of scripts in ModScript folder[/b][/color][/size][/i][/b][/*]
[*][b][i][size=3][b][i][size=3]MFM [/size][/i][/b]([u][b]_MapFileMaker)[/b][/u]: a [color=#ff0000][b]NEW console app to create MapFileTree files from EXMLs using drag/drop[/b][/color][/size][/i][/b][/*]
[/list][b]
[b][color=#ff0000][size=4]Installation: [/size][/color][/b][/b]
[list]
[*][b][size=3][color=#00ff00]See [/color][color=#00ff00][url=https://www.nexusmods.com/nomanssky/mods/2626][/url][/color][url=https://www.nexusmods.com/nomanssky/mods/2626]https://www.nexusmods.com/nomanssky/mods/2626[/url]﻿ [color=#00ff00]for detailed instructions![/color][/size][/b]
[b]* [color=#ffff00]Get the latest release from[/color] [url=https://github.com/HolterPhylo/AMUMSS/releases]AMUMSS latest releases on Github Link
[/url]* De-compress the zip file to its own folder (like C:\AMUMSS)   Making the path to AMUMSS main folder short helps prevent bumping against windows path length limit (in rare cases)
* No accented or special characters in the path
* Always extract to a new folder on any drive, never in any System, Documents or game folder
* Create an exception to AMUMSS main folder BEFORE executing anything
[/b][color=#ffff00][b]* If the cmd windows closes immediately (or is blank) when double-clicking BUILDMOD.BAT:
          if you are on windows 7.  Try making '-UseColors N' in BUILDMOD_AUTO.bat and use it instead of BUILDMOD.bat

[/b][/color][/*]
[/list][b]Amumss lua related support: [url=https://discord.gg/22ZAU9H]NMS Modding Discord[/url]? - see #amumss-lua channel
[/b]

name: Fast Actions 7.1
summary: Faster clicks and key pressed actions
author: Lo2k
description:
Instead of pressing a key for almost one second, this mod provides quicker or instant inputs for :
- menu selection
- creating/dismantling technology
- transfering resources to/from ship
- entering ship
- discussing with NPC
- gathering small plants
- launching refinery processing
- recovering deployable technologies

Enjoy !
[url=http://www.nexusmods.com/nomanssky/mods/28/?]
[/url]This mod exists in 2 versions :
﻿- instant actions : almost no delay actions (Fractal 4.13 added an in-game option but it only works for a limited range of actions)
- fast actions : between original and instant actions

[color=#ffd966]New : v7.1 fixes precise item quantity amount selection with Instant Actions. (Fast Action is untouched)[/color]


[size=2]Compatibility : [/size]
[url=https://www.nexusmods.com/nomanssky/mods/880][color=#ffd966]- [u]CHECK IF THIS MOD IS COMPATIBLE WITH LATEST GAME VERSION[/u][/color][/url]
[url=https://www.nexusmods.com/nomanssky/articles/85][color=#f6b26b]- [u]CHECK IF THIS MOD NEEDS TO BE MERGED WITH ANOTHER OF MY MODS[/u][/color][/url]

Credits : Many thanks to Shadwar for [b]the original version[/b]

name: Exosolar's and Babs' Beyond Base Building
summary: ★ FULLY MP compatible, this mod adds an extreme degree of freedom to the vanilla base-building options
author: Exosolar Sparky NeptuneX3 Babscoole
description:
[b][color=#f1c232][size=4]What is this Mod?[/size][/color][/b]
[size=4]There are now almost 1,000 buildable base parts in vanilla No Man's Sky! It's a dream come true!
This mod adds an extreme degree of freedom to the vanilla base-building options.
This does NOT mean the mod gives you access to all buildable parts.  You still need to learn the blueprints for each.  :)
Beyond Base Building does NOT add any new build parts to the game.

Now includes all the functionality of gBase Items, [color=#ff0000]if chosen by running the Lua script[/color]:[/size]

[list]
[*][size=4]Can multiply power, extraction, and storage rates by 2, 5, or 10. WARNING: Bases built with this mod cannot be uploaded for multiplayer when using 2x, 5x, or 10x multiplied rates.[/size][/*]
[/list]
[list]
[*][size=4]EM Generators do not need a hotspot and produce 250 power.  Not enabled by default.  Must run Lua through ANUMSS and answer question "Choose a multiplier for Storage and Rates and EMAnywhere status" to generate custom mod file to enable.[/size][/*]
[/list][size=4]

It is [b]fully compatible[/b] with multiplayer, co-op building, uploading, etc, because it [i]does not add[/i] any parts, which would cause issues in multiplayer.[/size]

[size=4][color=#f1c232][b]★ Beyond Basebuilding[/b] is fully Lua scripted, with script provided for easy updating and merging [b]★[/b][/color] and carries over all of the official updates and improvements offered by Hello Games [b]plus[/b]:[/size]

[list]
[*][size=4]Most parts can be Scaled [i][color=#fff2cc]except[/color][/i] prefab building parts, [color=#fff2cc]Exocraft Geobays, Mineral/Gas Extractors and Base Computer[/color] (Door attachment points bug out when scaled, and issues persist with scaled base computers and geobays).  There may be others not listed.  Be aware, scaling breaks snapping.[/size][/*]
[*][size=4]Many parts can be built in a Freighter.  [color=#fff2cc][i]Exceptions[/i][/color]  include the Base Computer, Industrial Mining parts, Vehicle Summoners and planet-based teleporter.[/size][/*]
[*][size=4]Many parts can be built at a Planetary Base [i][color=#fff2cc]except[/color][/i] the freighter teleporter and the Orbital Exocraft Materialiser[/size][/*]
[*][size=4]All parts can be built above and below water[/size][/*]
[*][size=4]All parts can change color and material[/size][/*]
[*][size=4]All parts, which can be deleted, can be deleted without also deleting attached objects [color=#fff2cc][i]except[/i][/color] Decals and Posters[/size][/*]
[*][size=4]No build-count limits [i][color=#fff2cc]except[/color][/i] for parts related to resources harvesting, certain terminals, beacons and message capsules (and others with limits that are hard coded into the game, as they are added or changed by Hello Games).[/size][/*]
[*][size=4]Unlimited Storage Containers[color=#cccccc][size=3][i]—*This does not increase actual storage amounts: Each numbered container acts as an index to the store rather than a physical box[/i][/size][/color][/size][/*]
[*][size=4]Foundation, Pavings and Communications Station can be placed anywhere (not only on bare terrain)[/size][/*]
[*][size=4]Non corvette parts buildable with corvettes[/size][/*]
[/list][size=4]
Some customization of the mod is available just by running it through AMUMSS, where you'll be prompted about enabling several feature.  Even more customization can be had by editing the top portion of the Lua before processing.  See Lua usage below for link to AMUMSS install and basic usage How to.[/size]

[color=#f1c232][b][u][size=5]Disclaimer:[/size][/u][/b][/color]
[size=3]This MOD makes changes to the placement and usage setting of vanilla parts.  If the MOD is removed, the vanilla settings will be restored and parts that "violate" a vanilla setting as well as those connected may go "poof".  Best practice is to regularly backup your saves for semi-painless recovery from the unexpected.[/size]

[center][img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/images/1634/95741288-1598421367.png[/img][/center]

[/u][/b][/size]

[size=4][b]✦[url=https://www.nexusmods.com/nomanssky/mods/2626]How to use AMUMSS at No Man's Sky Nexus - Mods and Community (nexusmods.com)[/url]✦[/b][/size][/b]

[size=5][u][b][color=#f1c232]Is this Mod up-to-date?[/color][/b][/u][/size]
[size=4][i][b]Any mod files which are not hidden or demoted to the "Old Files" area can be expected to work.[/b][/i]
All uploaded files (on the Files page) are maintained with their packing date and version of the MBINCompiler used to pack it. Older mod versions, no longer compatible with the current version of No Man's Sky will be demoted to the "Old Files" subsection, but will remain available for download. There are no side-effects to using this mod.[/size]

[size=4][u][b][color=#f1c232]How to update, tweak, combine, or patch the mod with AMUMSS?[/color][/b][/u][/size]
[size=4]As this mod is now Lua scripted, you can [color=#fff2cc]easily [b]update[/b], [b]tweak[/b] or [b]merge[/b] the mod yourself[/color] (if a game update breaks this PAK of the mod, or you have other PAK or Lua mods that could conflict with this one). [color=#cccccc][size=3][i]—*Click on the spoiler below for more[/i][/size][/color][/size]
[spoiler]
[size=3][u]Notes on AMUMSS:[/u]
[url=https://www.nexusmods.com/nomanssky/mods/957]AMUMSS[/url] tries to update [url=https://github.com/monkeyman192/MBINCompiler/releases]MBINcompiler[/url] automatically. If it fails to convert EXML/MBIN files, its likely due to a very recent NMS version update and MBINcompiler is awaiting an update too.
If so, I highly recommend you to disable your mods and wait for MBINCompiler to be updated (it's pretty quick in general).[/size]


[size=3][u][b][color=#f1c232]Updating the mod after a game update:[/color][/b][/u][/size]
[spoiler][list]
[*] [size=3]Download [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/nomanssky/mods/957]AMUMSS[/url], and extract it locally,  avoid placing the AMUMSS folder in a path with special characters or in a system folder like [color=#fff2cc][i]"C:\Program Files (x86)\(...)"[/i][/color].[/size][/*]
[*][size=3]Place the mod Lua script (only the .LUA file, not the .PAK file) in the [color=#fff2cc][i]ModScript[/i][/color] folder of AMUMSS.[/size][/*]
[*][size=3]AMUMSS will normally auto-detect the NMS installation folder, but you can also manually edit the path in the [color=#fff2cc][i]NMS_FOLDER.txt[/i][/color] file.[/size][/*]
[*][size=3]Launch [color=#fff2cc][i]BUILDMOD.bat[/i][/color] in the root of the AMUMSS folder and follow the steps, selecting the option to automatically install it in the NMS [color=#fff2cc][i]MODS[/i][/color] folder.[/size][/*]
[*][size=3]A new modded PAK file will be created, using the last update of the MBIN game files duly amended by the script.[/size][/*]
[/list]
[size=3]Most of the time simply recompiling the mod using the Lua script will make it compatible again. Use the latest script to ensure you adopt any mod adjustments to accommodate the latest NMS build.[/size][size=3] There are two main causes of mods not correctly updating with this method:
1) MBINCompiler needs an update to be able to decompile the latest version of the game MBIN files.
2) Structural changes have been made to the MBIN files called by this Lua script. Whilst unlikely, this will require some script rewrite and other mods are likely affected.[/size][/spoiler]

[size=3][u][b][color=#f1c232]Tweaking the mod:[/color][/b][/u][/size]
[spoiler][size=3]Use the same process as described in [color=#fff2cc][i]Updating the mod[/i][/color].
Just modify the script as you want before recompiling it. Look at the top of my scripts, you can generally edit modifiers there, or even disable/enable some features very easily.[/size]

[size=3][color=#cccccc][i]*[url=https://notepad-plus-plus.org/]Notepad++[/url] or [url=https://code.visualstudio.com/download/]VSCode[/url] are generally recommended to read EXML and Lua files, due to large file support and automatic highlighting. The [url=https://sourceforge.net/projects/npp-compare/]Compare plugin[/url] for Notepad++, and Lua is also a must-have to highlight file differences.[/i][/color][/size][/spoiler]

[size=3][u][b][color=#f1c232]Combining mods that provide Lua scripts:[/color][/b][/u][/size]
[spoiler][size=3]You'll want to do this if multiple mods affect the same MBIN file.[/size]

[list]
[*] [size=3]Place all the scripts in the [color=#fff2cc][i]ModScript[/i][/color] folder of AMUMSS.[/size][/*]
[*][size=3]Launch [color=#fff2cc][i]BUILDMOD.bat[/i][/color] in the root folder of AMUMSS.[/size][/*]
[*][size=3]Follow the step, selecting that you want to combine all the scripts into one PAK file, and the option to automatically install it in the NMS [color=#fff2cc][i]MODS[/i][/color] folder.[/size][/*]
[/list]
[size=3]AMUMSS will output a PAK file that will contain the modifications of all the scripts.[/size]
[color=#cccccc][size=3][i]*If the scripts modify the exact same values in the MBIN file, the last loaded script will overwrite the preceding script values.[/i][/size][/color][/spoiler]

[size=3][u][b][color=#f1c232]Patch in the mod to make it compatible with other PAK files:[/color][/b][/u][/size]
[spoiler][size=3]You will want to do this if another mods isn't Lua scripted, and affects the same MBIN file as the Lua scripted mod.

It will work when :
Case 1) you patch a Lua scripted mod for only one other PAK.
Case 2) you patch a Lua scripted mod for several other PAKs, but only if the other PAKs don't modify the same MBIN files between them.[/size]

[size=3][b]How to create a patched PAK:[/b][/size]

[list]
[*] [size=3]Copy the script and the other mod(s) PAK(s) in the [color=#fff2cc][i]ModScript[/i][/color] folder of AMUMSS.[/size][/*]
[*][size=3]Launch [color=#fff2cc][i]BUILDMOD.bat[/i][/color] in the root folder of AMUMSS, and follow the steps, selecting the option to automatically install it in the NMS [color=#fff2cc][i]MODS[/i][/color] folder.[/size][/*]
[*][size=3]The new PAK file will contain all the Lua script modifications, and a compatibility patch for the other mod(s) PAK(s).[/size][/*]
[*][size=3]Rename it something like [color=#fff2cc][i]"__zzz Scripted Mod Name -Patched For- The Other Mod Name.pak"[/i][/color].[/size]
[size=3][color=#fff2cc][i]"__zzz"[/i][/color] prefix is to ensure that it'll be loaded after the mod(s) it was patched for (for NMS loading order, underscore [i]"_"[/i] came after every other characters).[/size][/*]
[*][size=3]Remove the original scripted mod PAK from the [color=#fff2cc][i]MODS[/i][/color] folder, and copy both the new patched one, and the other mod(s) PAK(s), into it.[/size][/*]
[/list][size=3][u]Notes:[/u]
[b]Be sure the mod PAK you want to create a compatibility patch for, is updated for the latest NMS version.[/b]
-> When AMUSS find an MBIN file affected by a script and by a PAK in the [color=#fff2cc][i]ModScript[/i][/color] folder, it will take the MBIN file from the mod PAK over the original one of the game, and will apply to it the modifications listed in the script.
So, if the MBIN file of the mod PAK is not updated, the one in the resulting patched PAK won't be either.[/size][/spoiler]
[/spoiler]

[center][img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/images/1634/95741288-1598421367.png[/img][/center]
[color=#f1c232][u][b][size=5]Philosophy[/size][/b][/u][/color]
[size=4]All the erroneous limitations in building are pretty silly, aren't they? In making this mod, I paid specific attention to making sure I did not make changes that will invalidate the bases built with it from online play. Any base made with Beyond BaseBuilding will be fully viable and visible in online play, even when other players don't have this mod! It does this by only editing placement rules while you are in the building editor and not modifying any of the actual base parts themselves.
We hope you enjoy the mod and naturally, your comments are most welcome. We can also be found on the No Man's Sky Discord or reached directly at: [color=#f1c232]Babscoole #6326 [/color]or [color=#f1c232]Exosolar#5120[/color], or if its about the Lua script: [color=#f1c232]NeptuneX3#8095[/color] [size=3][i][size=4]or [/size][/i][/size][color=#f1c232]s p a r k y#0536[/color][/size]

files.changelog_html: Remove base build limit for wonder projector.

3.73: Updated to 3.73.  Some minor changes to the LUA for compatibility with AMUMSS 3.9.5.X+ syntax checking
3.81: Updated to 3.81.  Using a non-public nightly release of MBINCompiler, so fingers crossed.  :)
3.82: Update to 3.82.  Hopefully fixed the color issue, as all platforms are using the same game file version.
3.97: Update for 3.97.  First swing at Endurance compatibility.  Lots of stuff left to go over, so a work in progress
3.98: Update for 3.98.  Planter boxes now available on freighters.  Removed build limits to datasign.
3.99: Lots of Endurance "fixes".  GeoBays buildable on Freighters again (Change GEOBAYS_ON_FREIGHTER to true in LUA and run through AMUMSS, false by default).  Added back many other items by default as well.  Farm plants plantable in any biome.
4.03: Change to the LUA script only for use with AMUMSS 4.0.0.5.  Will be prompted in AMUMSS if you want GeoBays on freighters.  No changes to the mod itself
4.06: Update for 4.06.  Added Base storage containers to freighters, off by default.
4.08: Added FREIGHTERROOMS_ON_PLANETBASE.  By default disabled.  To enable, run the lua script through AMUMSS.  Say yes to enable global input.  When prompted, say Y to enable "Freighter Tech and Bio rooms on Planet Bases".  Overwrite old .pak with the new one generated.
4.38: Changes to the Lua ONLY.  Made some changes to prevent warnings/notices in AMUMSS when combining/merging with certain other base building mods.  Renamed the script to change AMUMSS processing order when combining/merging with certain other base building mods that could lead to a game crash.
4.45a: Removed planet build limits for Firepit and Spawner Ball
4.62: Update for Orbital.  Incorporates some changes from gBase Items
4.65a: MARINESHELTER_ABOVE_WATER now true by default.  Combined EMAnywhere and Multiplier selection into one prompt in the Lua
4.65b: Fixed typos in the multiplier prompt in the Lua script.  Geobays should now be scalable by default.  Fixed some notices when certain optional features were enabled via prompt when Lua is run.
4.72: Add Base Salvage Capsules on Freighters, add optional NPCSTATION_ON_FREIGHTER
5.26: Update for NMS 5.26.  Increase number of landing pads per planet base by 25%
5.28: Update for NMS 5.28.  Increase power range in freighters
5.28a: 2nd attempt at increasing free power range.  1st broke airlock door working, so reverting and trying from Bridge Connector.
5.29: Add anomaly seal (S16_HEATER) to freighter build
6.00: Compatibility for NMS 6.00 update.  Thee have been no changes dealing with corvette parts.  I'll look into that in coming days.
6.01: Hopefully added all parts not available in corvettes to corvettes.  I fully expect this to need to be dialed back a bit.
6.03: Fixes issue with double Short Range Teleporters
6.04: Update for NMS 6.04.  Added AMUMSS prompt to place Corvette parts on Planet Bases to script, so don't need to manually edit the script.  Default is Y
6.06a: Correct old typos in Lua script
6.23: Fixed a labelling error in the Lua script
6.33: Resolved issue with freighter tech rooms not showing up for some people in corvettes
6.34: Remove base build limit for wonder projector.

name: gBase - Automatic Proximity Doors - EXML
summary: Automatic doors and garages open and close like magic.
author: Gumsk
description:
[size=2]✅ Compatible - EXML patch mod - likely to work well with all other mods
✅ Update Safe - Likely to survive updates[/size]



[size=4]Did you recently start No Man's Sky, build a door, and can't figure out how to make it work? Are you using some kind of free power mod that powers your doors and you can't get them open?  Do you hate dealing with wires but want that sweet, sweet automatic door action? Then gDoor (pronounced G'door, like G'day) is for you![/size]

[size=4]There are four choices for this mod, which I've split out into different files so you can get just what you want.[/size]
[list]
[*][size=4]Proximity door - puts a proximity sensor into the automatic door so you don't have to wire it up to make it work. Just put it where you want it and get within 6u and it will open. That's it. Due to request, there's also an optional 2u version. If you have ideas for proximity other things, let me know.[/size]
[*][size=4]Proximity Garages - makes garage doors work without any wiring, like the proximity door. This works a bit farther out, 30u, so it will work in vehicles. The door opening speed has been sped up and the closing speed slowed down. There is an optional version with the vanilla speeds.[/size]
[/list]


[size=2]The proximity doors portion of the mod affects these files:
[/size][list]
[*][size=2]MODELS\PLANETS\BIOMES\COMMON\BUILDINGS\PARTS\BUILDABLEPARTS\DOOR\ENTITIES\MAINDOOR.ENTITY.MBIN[/size]
[*][size=2]MODELS\PLANETS\BIOMES\COMMON\BUILDINGS\PARTS\BUILDABLEPARTS\DOOR.SCENE.MBIN[/size]
[*][size=2]MODELS\PLANETS\BIOMES\COMMON\BUILDINGS\PARTS\BUILDABLEPARTS\BASICPARTS\MESHES\COMMON\ENTITIES\GARAGEDOOR.ENTITY.MBIN[/size]
[/list]
[b]You can find information about and links to all my mods, files affected by each of my mods, instructions for installing mods, and instructions for extracting LUA files here:[/b]
[url=https://www.nexusmods.com/nomanssky/mods/1541/]https://www.nexusmods.com/nomanssky/mods/1541/[/url]

name: Better Rewards
summary: Increase the amount of currency you get with faster word learning and increased resource gathering options. Frigate (Freighter) Expeditions are also included!
author: MrTrack
description:
Better Rewards increases the amount of units, nanite clusters and quicksilver you get from completing missions or interacting with objects in the world. Non npc word learning (such as knowledge stones and dictionaries) is also faster.

[color=#bf9000][size=3]IMPORTANT NOTE: Don't use this mod when I haven't updated it to the new game version (especially when it is a new or returning expedition)
[/size][/color][color=#bf9000]if you really want to use it when I haven't updated it yet, make sure you [/color][color=#bf9000]back up your save[/color][color=#bf9000] or build the mod using the lua scripts yourself.[/color]

[b]Currency[/b]

Currency gain is roughly increased by 5 times, so as to make it a viable option besides crafting and selling valuable trading goods. Some unit values were tremendously increased (eg.: from minimum amount of 1000-2000 to 20000-50000) like the terminals in abandoned settlements to make the early game less grindy.

Includes:
[list]
[*]Space station missions and Nexus missions
[*]Interactable objects/devices (abandoned buildings, damaged machinery, waypoints, etc.)
[*]Sentinels
[*]Alien interactions
[/list]
[b]Words[/b]

Learning words from non npc interactions (knowledge stones and dictionaries) is also 3 times faster (3 words learned form a single knowledge stone). You now also learn 5 Atlas words, instead of 1 and also have a chance (15%) to learn them when interacting with non npc learning sources.

[b]Resources

[/b]When gathering with the mining beam you generally get 2 times more resources (but only when the things explode as you mine them). Also includes containers, depots, guild rewards, asteroids and ships that drop resources.

[b]Frigate (Freighter) Expedition Rewards

[/b]After a successful expedition, when collecting your rewards you get 5x more units and nanites, 2x more products like Storage Augmentation and NipNip Buds and 3x more resources like Copper, Emeril and Cadmium.
[b]
Installation

[/b]Make sure you delete or edit DISABLEMODS.TXT in your game library (...\Steam\steamapps\common\No Man's Sky\GAMEDATA\PCBANKS) before trying to use mods. Extract the zip file to ...\Steam\steamapps\common\No Man's Sky\GAMEDATA\MODS folder and you are good to go (Create MODS folder if you don't have one). Alternatively you can just use Vortex to download and install.

This mod modifies the [color=#ff0000][size=3]REWARDTABLE.MBIN[/size][/color] and [color=#ff0000][size=3]EXPEDITIONREWARDTABLE.MBIN[/size][/color] file and will not work with other mods that use those tables.

[b]Updated to NMS version 6.21 (Build ID: 21918502) on 2026.02.13.[/b]

name: NomNom
summary: NomNom is the most complete savegame editor for NMS but also shows you additional information around the data you're about to change. You can also easily look up each item individually to examine its attributes, independently of a savegame, or get other useful information that are not related to a specific one (but enhanced if one is loaded).
author: zencq
description:
[color=#e69138][size=3][b]NomNom is now in beta state.[/b] This means for you that some things may still not be 100% finished but the general state of the tool is a lot better than before.[/size] If you encounter bugs or when you think something doesn't work as expected or could be improved, [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/nomanssky/mods/1566?tab=bugs]let me know[/url]![/color]

[color=#ff0000]All available features are tested while implemented but mistakes can happen, so be aware of that and backup your saves![/color]

The features can be divided into the different main parts:
[list]
[*][b]Manager[/b] Manage your saves![list]
[*]Automatic backup.
[*]Save your save as human-readable JSON.
[*]Copy, move, and swap slots to another.
[*]Delete your save.
[/list]
[*][b]Editor[/b] Edit your save for your needs![list]
[*]Ability to manage fleets, change data values like currencies and stats, and organize inventories.
[*]Ability to fast travel to any system you like, trigger space battles, and clear portal interference.
[*]Ability to edit your knowledge and recorded experiences.
[/list]
[/list]
Have a look at the screenshots as well, to see what NomNom is capable of.

Each version of NomNom is built with the latest game version (at that time) in mind and the version number is reflecting that. Each one is also backwards
compatible down to [b]Beyond 2.11[/b] and even saves before that can still be edited via the build-in JSON editor. If you use it with a newer game version branch than
the one it was built for (e.g. [b]7.00[/b]), things should work as usual in most cases but there can always be a breaking change.

NomNom is compatible with every platform No Man's Sky is available on (some work better than others though). You can select a specific account/folder with you saves or, for platforms on PC, one level above if you have multiple accounts. A complete and always up-to-date overview with additional notes can be found [url=https://github.com/zencq/libNOM.io?tab=readme-ov-file#getting-started]on GitHub[/url].


[b]Instructions[/b][list=1]
[*]After downloading and extracting the zip file you'll find a few files. All you need to care about is the executable ([b]NomNom.exe[/b]) but new folders may    be created at runtime (e.g. for backups) that will become relevant to you.
[*]You may want to create a shortcut to the executable for easier access.
[*]As the tool includes an automatic backup functionality you don't need to do it manually but if you want to be absolutely safe, do it.
[*]At first start the tool locates your saves at the default location of each platform but if that fails, you must select it manually.
[*]Select a slot.
[*]Start tinkering!
[/list]

If you like NomNom and want to support me in its further development, you can do so via the donation options above or on [url=https://www.buymeacoffee.com/cengelha]Buy Me a Coffee[/url], [url=https://github.com/sponsors/cengelha]GitHub[/url], [url=https://ko-fi.com/cengelha]Ko-fi[/url], and [url=https://www.patreon.com/cengelha]Patreon[/url]. I will appreciate it!
There won't be a paywall though.

The is also a dedicated [url=https://discord.gg/nomnom-762409407488720918]Discord[/url] server where you'll find all information in one place, firsthand. Please read the rules first, to gain full access. If you need (or want to offer) help, found a bug, have a suggestion, or something else regarding NomNom, you will find, or can post it there.

Issues created here on Nexus Mods or via another channel will of course also be addressed.


Header image by [url=https://patreon.com/Orioto]Orioto[/url]﻿.

3.1.0.16-alpha.16: fixed save incompatibilities due to game updates once and for all (except there are some real breaking changes) and added some nice improvements for Windows Store and PlayStation platforms
3.5.0.17-alpha.17: database update for Prisms 3.53, added JSON editor and platform save transfer
3.6.0.18-alpha.18: compatibility with the new save streaming system
3.6.0.19-alpha.19: compatibility with the new save streaming system for PlayStation
3.7.0.20-alpha.20: fixed knowledge tab, and added cross-save and companion editing
3.7.0.21-alpha.21: renamed editor buttons to avoid confusion, fixed some issues (with the Microsoft platform)
4.00.0.31-beta.1: now in beta state with countless new features and improvements incl. update for Waypoint 4.00 and support for Nintendo Switch
4.40.0.34-beta.4: update for Echoes 4.40 and lots of bug fixes
4.50.2.37-beta.7: update for Omega 4.50 incl. support for new Xbox save format, bug fixes
4.70.1.40-beta.10: bug fixes, experimental support for new save format
5.00.0.44-beta.13: update for Worlds Part I 5.00, bug fixes
5.20.2: update for The Cursed 5.27, bug fixes, underfill inventory, show save names in manager
5.50.0: update for Worlds Part II 5.50, bug fixes

name: JinxterMODs Multitool HUD Reborn
summary: JinxsterMOD's Multitool HUD Ported into Frontiers Update
author: Virtual Voyager - JinxterMOD
description:
[b]Update:[/b] Still working for [b]Frontiers[/b]

I was looking for a good multitool HUD retexture and couldn't find any, so I found a good one and ported it. Also included is my modification of the original file to suit my own taste.

[b]Credits:[/b] All credits for this mod go to [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/nomanssky/users/37600940]JinxterMOD[/url]
[b]
Notable Mention: [/b]Thanks to user [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/nomanssky/users/87374]Lo2k[/url] for giving me resources on how to save DDS files in No Mans Sky.

[/b]TEXTURES/UI/WEAPON/[b]GUNSCREEN.DDS[/b]
TEXTURES/UI/WEAPON/[b]LASERSCREEN.DDS[/b]

name: Companion Pet Unlocker
summary: Allows the possibility to add almost any creature as a pet.
author: Mjjstral aka MetaIdea and Babscoole
description:
[color=#ff0000][b][size=4]Join our friendly and helpfull [size=5][url=https://discord.gg/AEXcap6]NMS Discord Community[/url][/size] with a special focus on ship seeds and creatures, modding, savegames, 3d printing, exploration and game tips and much more ;)[/size][/b][/color]
[size=5]
To tame air and sea creatures you must be on foot[/size]. That's a limitation of the game so wait till the air creature flys low or the sea creature is close to the shore. Or build a plattform in or above the water, marine shelter,  or a air tower.  See the videos on the video tab for successful techniques.

FAQ:
[list]
[*]unlocked creatures can lay eggs[/*]
[*]unlocked creatures can be milked[/*]
[*]More creatures can now be ridden[/*]
[*]other players can see them too[/*]
[/list]

[b][size=6]Install:  Extract archive contents to GAMEDATA\MODS[/size]

Mod Description:

[/b]15 Nov 2022:  Now includes reduction of pet egg times

16 Feb 2022:  Tutorial added on Video Tab for capturing/taming Flying Lizards, courtesy of Zelutherian.

8 July 2021:  v3.53a.  Adds the ability to ride some creatures that couldn't be ridden before, thanks to Corvus

Also slimmed the LUA a little via function/tables/loops.  Big thanks go out to Silent369, who is now credited as a mod Contributor.  Be aware the mod now makes multiple changes to GCCREATUREGLOBALS.MBIN.
4.13: Update for 4.13.  Added prompt to lua, when run through AMUMSS, to not add pet egg timer changes.
4.40: Update to 4.40 Echoes.  Refactoring to Lua script.
5.58: Update for NMS 5.58, Worlds Pt II.  101 files getting the EXML treatment, fingers crossed.
5.70: Update for NMS 5.70, Beacon.  Added Squid

name: Feed your Living Ship delicious abyssal treats
summary:
Your Living Ship is HUNGRY. Overhauls Living Ship launch systems. It can be charged with delectable dishes like Abyssal Stew, Stellar Ice Cream, 'Cake of Sin.
Standalone component of my larger mod, THE TRAVELLER (https://www.nexusmods.com/nomanssky/mods/1929)

Very easy to add to game, see "MOD Installation".
author: hilightnotes
description:
// [url=https://youtu.be/MojDoeloUTc]Learning from those who preceded us[/url] .. [url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=llci8MVh8J4&feature=emb_logo]<3[/url] .. [url=https://youtu.be/UGoUSNTafRU]♫♫[/url] .. [url=https://twitter.com/hilightnotes/status/1267043072107569153]Positive change requires disruption[/url]﻿﻿
[b]__________________________________________________
[/b]
Documentation for the full mod, The Traveller, [url=https://docs.google.com/document/d/1Bq6IDVGUXRreExlvrD-X52BxxJuVOExPmzlY5bUHQQQ/edit?usp=sharing]found here[/url]

[size=5]This is a standalone component of my larger mod called "[url=https://www.nexusmods.com/nomanssky/mods/1929]THE TRAVELLER[/url]"[/size][size=4][url=https://youtu.be/13em4JnJX0k]
[/url][/size]

[color=#6aa84f]Living Ship Launch Systems[/color] are more alive than ever, and they're HUNGRY!
- Launch requirements are different. Launching will use up more fuel. Oxygen can still be used to recharge, but is not nearly as effective a fuel source and are recommended only as a last resort. (After all,  your Living Ship needs some real nutrients to keep its Neural Assembly comfy and operational!).
- [color=#f1c232]Charge the Neural Assembly (launch thrusters) with larval cores, hypnotic eyes, or all manner of abyssal/stellar dishes[/color]. Cooked dishes will be particularly effective.
- Upgrades to Neural Assembly are unchanged, but more impactful due to the altered launch systems, providing more sense of progression.

[size=4]Look, the important thing here is that you can now feed your Living Ship a Doomed Cream Cake.[/size]
For help with cooking,[url=https://nomanssky.fandom.com/wiki/Cooking] take a look at this wiki page[/url].

[b]
[size=3]Go to the Files tab on NexusMods [/size][/b][size=3](this page)[/size][b] to download the mod.
[/b]Includes LUA for merging.

If you want these changes as part of a comprehensive package to improve the exploration and immersive experience of No Man's Sky, use [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/nomanssky/mods/1929]THE TRAVELLER[/url] instead.
____________________________________________
____________________________________________
____________________________________________

[size=2]Further[/size] reading,[b][size=5] 
table of contents:[/size][/b]

[b][size=3]A.[color=#f1c232] [u]Mod Installation
[/u][/color][/size][/b][b][size=3][b][size=3]B. [/size][u][size=3]List of Changes[/size][/u][/b]
C. [u][b][u]Compatibility[/u][/b][/u][/size]
[size=3]D. [/size][b][u][size=3]Credits/thanks[/size]
[/u][/b][size=3]E. [b][u]Support me![/u][/b][/size][/b]


____________________________________________
____________________________________________
____________________________________________


[b][size=4]A. [/size][u][color=#f1c232][size=4]MOD Installation[/size][/color]
[/u]
[/b]This mod installs the same way as any regular mod for No Man's Sky. It's very simple! Extract the mod to your mod folder. 
[b]
If this is your first time installing a mod for No Man's Sky,[color=#f1c232] it takes 1 minute.[/color][/b][b][color=#f1c232] Follow these steps:[/color][/b]

[b]1[/b]. Navigate to your game folders. For me (Steam user), that means I go to:
C:\Program Files (x86)\Steam\steamapps\common\No Man's Sky\GAMEDATA\PCBANKS
[b]2[/b]. Delete the file DisableMods.txt
[b]3[/b]. In that same folder (PCBANKS), create a new folder for your mods. It can be called whatever you want, I just call it "MODS". [url=https://i.imgur.com/38kXMmP.png]https://i.imgur.com/38kXMmP.png[/url]
[b]4[/b]. Download the mod from the Files tab here on nexusmods. It will download as a zip file. Extract the .pak file contained within the .zip file (I use 7-zip) into the MODS folder you created.
[b]5[/b]. All set! (When the game starts you will get a screen that warns you that you are using mods).
[b]
Deleting the .pak file fully uninstalls the mod.[/b] You can leave the mod folder you've created.


[b]Sometimes when the game updates, it will recreate the DisableMods.txt[/b]. Just delete it again to continue using mods.
[b]Sometimes when the game updates (usually just major updates), the mod will be temporarily incompatible.[/b] I update the mod within 1-14 days depending on circumstances, at which point you just replace the old version with the new version to keep using the mod.


____________________________________________


[b][size=4]B. [/size][/b][u][b][size=4]List of Changes:[/size]
[/b][/u]
[size=3][b]v1.0[/b]
[/size]- [color=#f1c232]Living Ships are more alive than ever[/color]. You can still use Oxygen to fuel your Living Ship, however you'll need a LOT more Oxygen for a single launch than before (lets get that fellow some real nutrients!?!? Geez...). Instead, you'll be wanting to [color=#f1c232]feed your Living Ship all manner of Abyssal treats[/color]. The bread and butter fuel option are larval cores, a simple but delectable snack for Living Ships. For a more efficient and thoughtful care of your alien kin, it is recommended to cook for it. Abyssal Stew is a healthy classic, or if you're feeling a little cheeky you might feed it a Cake of Sin. All manner of abyssal meals can be fed to it, and comfortably satiate its needs for launching.

- Living Ship Neural Assembly stats have been balanced to fit the vision of the mod.

- Relevant products can now be used to charge the Living Ship Neural Assembly (launch thrusters).


Remember [color=#f1c232]this mod is non-destructive, and can be removed at any time with no consequence[/color]. I don't expect issues/bugs but please let me know if you encounter any.


____________________________________________


[size=4][b]D. [/b][u][b]Compatibility[/b][/u][/size]

When you have multiple mods editing the same files, they will conflict. [color=#f1c232]You can run a compatibility check against any other mods you're using by [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/nomanssky/mods/957]downloading AMUMSS[/url]﻿, following the setup, and running the BUILDMOD WindowsBatchFile[/color]. Just press 'y' on your keyboard when it prompts to check for any conflicts in your mods folder.

If you're feeling brave you can also learn how to merge mods.

[b]This mod edits a few files that will conflict with some other mods, particularly ones that adjust technologies, products, or ship functionality. Listed below are the edited files.[/b]

NMS_REALITY_GCTECHNOLOGYTABLE.MBIN
NMS_REALITY_GCPRODUCTTABLE.MBIN


This mod should work smoothly! Please let me know if you run into any issues.
Please feel free to let me know if there is something about the mod you would like changed or improved.

____________________________________________


[b][size=4]E. [/size][u][size=4]Credits/thanks
[/size]
[/u][/b]Big thanks to the NMS modding community for the tools they've created to make modding more accessible. Thanks also to the "No Man's Sky Modding" Discord community, where I was able to ask questions to help me figure some things out.

____________________________________________


[b][size=4]F. [/size][u][size=4]Support me!
[/size]
[/u][/b]If you like this mod and feel like being awesome, send a little my way!
Venmo: @Noam-Bergman
Paypal: [url=https://www.paypal.me/noambergman]https://www.paypal.me/noambergman

[/url]Also supports my ongoing creative work at [url=https://youtube.com/hilightnotes]youtube.com/hilightnotes[/url].

author: WinderTP
description:
[center][size=5]HI, WINDERTP HERE FOR[/size]
[font=Verdana][size=6][b][u]CONSUMERISM™

[/u][/b][/size][/font][size=5]THE EASIEST WAY TO BUY, BUY, BUY YOUR WAY TO SUCCESS

[/size][size=4][b]CONSUMERISM™[/b] LETS YOU BUY ANYTHING, EVERYTHING* IN [b]THE QUICKSILVER SHOP[/b]

ARE YOU A PERSON WHO DOESN'T CONTRIBUTE TO THE COMMUNITY
BUT WANTS TO REAP THE REWARDS BEFORE ANYONE ELSE?
GET [b]CONSUMERISM™[/b] TODAY AND GET A HEAD START[/size]
[img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/1634/images/2062/2062-1630866855-250424957.jpeg[/img]﻿

[size=4]ARE YOU A PERSON WHO DOESN'T HAVE THE TIME TO WATCH CONTENT CREATORS
BUT HAVE THE CAPITAL TO BANKRUPT THE QUICKSILVER SYNTHESIS COMPANION?
GET [b]CONSUMERISM™[/b] TODAY AND FEEL GOOD ABOUT YOURSELF[/size]
[img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/1634/images/2062/2062-1630866865-1581207615.jpeg[/img]﻿

[size=4]ARE YOU A PERSON WHO DOESN'T DRIVE A DELOREAN
AND THEREFORE CANNOT GO BACK IN TIME TO FINISH A TIMED EVENT
THAT NOT EVERYBODY HAS THE TIME TO FINISH IN A TIMELY MANNER AND YOU WERE TOO OUTATIME TO DO?
GET [b]CONSUMERISM™[/b] TODAY AND BUY ALL THE TITLES YOU COULD ASK FOR[/size]
[img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/1634/images/2062/2062-1630866874-380858041.jpeg[/img]﻿

[size=6]EVEN SPACE IS CORRUPTED BY CAPITALISM[/size]
[size=4]GET [b]CONSUMERISM™[/b] TODAY FOR FREE
AND START SPENDING YOUR HARD-EARNED QUICKSILVER
[/size](Lua available for auto-update and adding more products to the Quicksilver shop)[/center]
[center][url=https://nomanssky.fandom.com/wiki/Mods#Installing_Mods][size=6][b]Installing Mods Manually[/b][/size][/url]﻿﻿[/center][size=6][center][url=https://stepmodifications.org/wiki/NoMansSky:Tutorials/HowToAMUMSS]How To merge with AMUMSS[/url]

[url=https://www.nexusmods.com/nomanssky/mods/957][size=5][b][center]Click here to go to AMUMSS's Nexus mod page.[/center][/b][/size][/url]
