name: Bigger Creature Spawn range
summary: This will double the spawn range for all creatures and also ups the spawn ratio for cave animals slightly as they are annoyingly hard to find.
author: Mina
description:
[font=Tahoma][color=#d5a6bd]Hi everybody,

I always used a creature spawn range mod in my playthroughs however I could not find one which was compatible with the current version of the game 3.6 Frontiers.
So I made one by myself. This is my first Mod, so don't be too harsh on me. I have creatures spawning now up to 400-500u away. I tried to keep it vanilla friendly and not cheaty as some other creature spawn rate mods, so you won't get more or more frequent animals (bare the cave animals because those are annoying af), you can only catch them farther away. You probably still need to walk towards them, as some animals that spawned ~400u away in my game were hidden behind hills etc.[/color][/font][font=Tahoma][color=#c27ba0][color=#d5a6bd] I playtested this yesterday for roughly 4-5 hours, I didn't found any bugs or issues, all animals spawned, and it was a little easier to complete them.[/color]
[b][size=4]
What does this mod do?[/size]
[/b][color=#d5a6bd]- I doubled the spawn range from 1x to 2x for all animal types who got 1x as a standard ratio. Also upped the ones from 1.25x to 2x. The few water animals who start with 5x by default already, I left alone.
- I upped the chance for some cave animals to spawn. 0.25 Chance got upped to 0.5, 0.5 got upped to 0.75 and 0.75 to 1, so they should be a little less grindy to find, but the rare ones are still challenging to find and need a little patience.[/color]
[size=4]
[b]Which game version is this compatible with?[/b][/size]
[color=#d5a6bd]Ibuilt and tested the mod with the current version v3.68 on PC. It should be compatible with every version from v3.6 onwards, as long as the files which the mod modifies are not changed or moved.[/color]

[b][size=4]Which files are modified?[/size]
[/b][color=#d5a6bd]Nearly every file in
\METADATA\SIMULATION\ECOSYSTEM\AIR
[font=Tahoma] \METADATA\SIMULATION\ECOSYSTEM\CAVE
[font=Tahoma] \METADATA\SIMULATION\ECOSYSTEM\DEPRECATE
[font=Tahoma] \METADATA\SIMULATION\ECOSYSTEM\GROUND
[font=Tahoma] \METADATA\SIMULATION\ECOSYSTEM\UNDERWATER[/font][/font][/font]
[/font] minus the Sentinel table and a few empty tables and unused ones.[/color]

[b][size=4]Can you do a .lua version?[/size]
[/b][color=#d5a6bd]I don't know how to do this, but in case anybody needs one, and I have a little freetime, I'll try to look into it. In case anybody else want to do it, just write me a message here - I'll upload it and credit you accordingly.[/color]

[b][size=4]Can you modify other values?[/size]
[/b][color=#d5a6bd]If there is demand, I'll do it. However mind you, it'll get cheaty really fast.[/color]


[b][i]
All Donationpoints go to Doctors without Borders. <3[/i][/b]
[/color][/font]

name: No Knowledge Stones In The Wild 2.0
summary: Removes Knowledge Stones from planets
author: Lo2k
description:
This mod was suggested to me by Agh1353 and I believe it deserves existing for those who want to get rid of these weird stones scattered on each and every planet surface.

So here it is.

Enjoy.

Options:
- Also remove Buried Technologies with [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/nomanssky/mods/1774][color=#ffd966]No Buried Technologies[/color]
[/url]- Also remove Damaged Machineries with [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/nomanssky/mods/1649][color=#ffd966]No Damaged Machineries[/color][/url]
- Also remove Cargo Drops with [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/nomanssky/mods/1307][color=#ffd966]No Cargo Drops[/color][/url]﻿


[b]This mod has been updated for Worlds Part 2 new modding scheme. [/b][color=#ffd966]v2.0 has been updated for 5.58 game version and improves compatibility.[/color]

name: Quick Damaged Machineries 2.8
summary: 1-click no junk damaged machineries
author: Lo2k
description:
In the same vein as my [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/nomanssky/mods/1162][color=#ffd966]Quick Crates[/color][/url] mod, this mod removes junk from Damaged Machineries to be able to open them in 1 click.
A simple change but a great time saving !
The only draw back for now is that machineries are permanently opened but I hope one day the opening animation could be restored.


[b]This mod has been updated for Worlds Part 2 new modding scheme. [/b][color=#ffd966]v2.8 has been updated for 5.58 game version and improves compatibility.[/color]

name: Seed Display Tool - Seed Hunters Eyes
summary: A unique tool that enables seed value display for ships, multitools, pets, plants and npcs. A convenient way to collect procedural things ingame and quickly share them.
author: Mjstral aka MetaIdea
description:
A unique tool that enables seed value display for ships, multitools, pets, plants and npcs ingame. A convenient way to collect procedural things ingame and quickly share them. (You can also Download it for FREE [url=https://www.patreon.com/posts/69469200]here [/url]if you have problems downloading it on nexus or if you want to support the author)

It displays the seed in your visor screen as well as above the procedural thing when you are in interaction range (see images).

What can you do with the seeds ? The seed values can be used to archive the procedural thing as they implicitely contain all infos about the thing like parts, body, colors, textures, decals used. They can later be used to add the thing into your savegame with any saveeditor.

I made this tool so you can quickly share the things (images) with it's seed on our worldwide largest seed database discord server with 25.000 members on:

[size=5][url=https://discord.gg/AEXcap6]DISCORD TO SHARE AND FIND SEEDS: discord.gg/AEXcap6[/url][/size]

If you set the image filename to the seed like "0x123456.png" and upload it in our channels, the seed will be detected by our bot and added as text below your image post.

Also check out the revolutionary [b]No Man's Sky Ship Customizer on [size=4][url=https://nms.center/]www.nms.center[/url].

I hope you like this tool, it was super hard work to create it.

Last update: 11.April.2024: NMS Orbital

[size=3]
[/size][/size][/b][size=3]Installation is very simple:
- Right click on ENABLE-RUN-AS-ADMIN.bat and click run as admin.

Deinstallation:
- Right click on DISABLE-RUN-AS-ADMIN.bat and click run as admin.[/size][b][size=4]
[/size][/b]
Since this is not a regular pak archive based mod, don't use vortex mod manager.

author: Gumsk
description:

✅ Update Safe - Likely to survive updates[/size]



[size=4]This mod fully unlocks purple system access for your character, the same as if you had finished the new questline. You should play the storyline, but if you can't, here you go. Instructions
[/size][list=1]
[*][size=4]Install the mod, as you would other mods.[/size]
[*][size=4]Open your quick menu.[/size]
[*][size=4]Go to emotes.[/size]
[*][size=4]Blow your mind with the Unlock Purple emote.[/size]
[*][size=4]Save.[/size]
[*][size=4]Remove the mod.[/size]
[/list][size=4]
The first time you open the galaxy map, it will seem frozen as it slowly reveals the purple stars to you. Just wait.[/size]

[size=2]

Put the entire contents of the zip file in your GAMEDATA\MODS folder, [i]not [/i]PCBANKS, similar to this:
[img]https://i.ibb.co/ZRHHnjJK/image.png[/img]

You can find information about and links to all my mods, files affected by each of my mods, instructions for installing mods, and instructions for extracting LUA files here:
[url=https://www.nexusmods.com/nomanssky/mods/1541/]https://www.nexusmods.com/nomanssky/mods/1541/[/url][/size]

name: MC Character Customiser
summary: Replace the SavePoint tech by a fonctionnal CharacterCustomiser and add it to the known tech on new saves
author: CRealEmSay
description:
[b]MC Character Customiser[/b][i]A Portable Appearance Modifier for No Man’s Sky[/i]📜 [b]Description[/b]Tired of running back and forth between stations and planets just to change your appearance or equip an aquatic helmet? [i]MC Character Customiser[/i] offers a [b]QoL solution[/b] by making the [b]Appearance Modifier portable[/b]. No more searching for a terminal—get instant access anywhere!⚙️ [b]Technical Details[/b]This mod modifies the following files:[list]
[*][code]METADATA\GAMESTATE\DEFAULTSAVEDATA.MBIN[/code]
[*][code]MODELS\PLANETS\BIOMES\COMMON\BUILDINGS\PARTS\BUILDABLEPARTS\TECH\SAVEPOINT\ENTITIES\SAVEPOINT.ENTITY.MBIN[/code]
[/list]🛠️ [b]Installation[/b][list=1]
[*]Download and extract the mod.
[*]Place the extracted folder in:
[code]No Man's Sky\GAMEDATA\MODS[/code]
[*]Make sure [b]mods are enabled[/b] by deleting or renaming the [code]DISABLEMODS.TXT[/code] file.
[/list]🤝 [b]Why this mod?[/b][quote]I was tired of running between stations and planets just to change my appearance or equip an aquatic helmet. I see this as a QoL improvement, so I’m sharing it.

This mod does not add any new technology, it simply replace the SavePoint tech by a fonctionnal CharacterCustomiser and add it to the known tech on new saves.[/quote] Enjoy and have fun! 🚀

name: All Icons
summary: Global replacement of game icons!
author: Phantom
description:
[color=#6aa84f][b]Collection of replacement icon.[/b][/color]
[color=#6aa84f]      [/color]
[b]The purpose of fashion:[/b]

Our favorite game gets prettier every year. But such an aspect as the game icons leaves much to be desired. I fixed this situation. With the help of two of my mods, you can replace almost all the icons in the game with more detailed ones and in better quality. I spent many hours selecting and adjusting the sizes and colors. Some of the items in the game, as well as their descriptions, do not exist in real life. I had to use my imagination). I tried to follow the game style, or at least the color of the replacement icons, for a smoother transition.
[b]
[/b][b][color=#6aa84f]Changed:[/color][/b] 86 textures
[b][color=#6aa84f]Added:[/color][/b] 2 textures
Some elements in the game have the same icon. The mod adds its own texture to each item. If there was a duplication.

[color=#6aa84f][b]Recommendation Mod:[/b][/color]

[color=#1e84cc][url=http://www.nexusmods.com/nomanssky/mods/3070]Substation icons[/url]﻿     [/color][color=#6aa84f]        [/color]
[color=#1e84cc][url=http://www.nexusmods.com/nomanssky/mods/1277?tab=files]Colorless Inventories[/url]﻿[/color][color=#6aa84f]    [/color]
[color=#1e84cc][url=http://www.nexusmods.com/nomanssky/mods/3458]Borderless Icons[/url]﻿ [/color]

[b][color=#6aa84f]Compatibility.[/color][/b]

All versions of the game. The mod contains only textures.

[b][color=#6aa84f]Installation.[/color][/b]

Install like any no man's sky's mods :

Create a folder called MODS in the folder <No man's sky installation directory>/GAMEDATA/
Extract the content of the zip file in <No man's sky installation directory>/GAMEDATA/MODS/!!!

name: Bulk Craft (CE Table)
summary:
Quickly craft thousands of items using the available materials.
Press F1 to add 10x, F2 to add 100x and F3 to add x1000
Shift+Fkey to reduce the number
(you need to press D at least once before using the Fkeys)
author: devaway
description:
[size=4]**Not a cheat, you need the materials in order to complete the craft**[/size]
[size=1]**PLEASE LET ME KNOW IF IT WORKS IN YOUR COMPUTER. LEAVE A QUICK COMMENT :)**
[/size]
[list]
[*][b]F1[/b] → Add [b]×10[/b]
[*][b]F2[/b] → Add [b]×100[/b]
[*][b]F3[/b] → Add [b]×1000[/b]
[*][b]Shift + F-key[/b] → Subtract the same amount
[*]⚠️ You must press D on the item at least once before using the hotkeys, or it won’t work.
[/list]
Requirements✅ [b]Cheat Engine is required.[/b]
Download it from the [url=https://www.cheatengine.org/]official website[/url] or [url=https://github.com/cheat-engine/cheat-engine]GitHub[/url].
[line]How to Install[list=1]
[*]Install Cheat Engine.
⚠️ During installation, [b]decline any offers for third-party software[/b] (like extra browsers or tools).

[*]Download the .ct
file from this page.

[*]Open it with Cheat Engine (Ctrl + O)
or double-click if associated with Cheat Engine).

[*]The table should [b]auto-attach to the NMS process.[/b]

[*]Jump into the game, press[b] D[/b]
[b] on the item you want to craft[/b], then use the hotkeys.

[/list][line]⚠️ Notes[list]
[*]This is my [b]first Cheat Engine table.[/b] If the game updates and it breaks, I’ll try to fix it, but no promises.
[/list]
[b]IS IT SAFE?[/b]
You can open the .ct file with a text editor and check the source code. It's fine to use cheat engine on NMS just be careful when using with other games
Also, when installing from their official site, pay attention to the installer setup and decline when it asks if you want to install extra stuff (like 3rd party browsers)

name: JE's Asteroid Mining Flyby Begone II
summary: Removes the NPC Asteroid Miner flyby that occurs when you are mining asteroids, because he's annoying and loves to jump into your line of fire like a doofus.
author: Jaggid Edje and Babscoole
description:
I rarely mine asteroids, but when I do I find the NPC mining ship that shows up very annoying, as he loves to jump in front of your line of fire, and then goes hostile....but shooting him back pisses off sentinels.   This mod should remove the "Mining flyby" entirely to prevent that.

Updated [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/nomanssky/mods/2633]JE's Asteroid Mining Flyby Begone[/url] for current NMS.  With permission from the original author, Jaggid Edje, without which this mod would not be possible.  

Installation:
Extract archive contents to GAMEDATA\MODS

Files modified:
METADATA\SIMULATION\SCENE\EXPERIENCESPAWNTABLE.MBIN

For install and usage of AMUMSS to self update the mod or combine with other mods using the included Lua script please see [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/nomanssky/mods/2626]AMUMSS How To[/url]

[b]#INSTALL#[/b]
Download and Extract archive contents
Place entire "BuildableShip 99999 EXML" folder inside [u][b]GAMEDATA\MODS\[/b][/u]
the full path looks like this:
D:\Steam\SteamApps\Common\No Man's Sky\GAMEDATA\MODS\BuildableShip 99999 EXML\GLOBALS\GCBUILDABLESHIPGLOBALS.GLOBAL.EXML

[b]#UNINSTALL#[/b]
Delete entire "BuildableShip 99999 EXML" folder inside [u][b]GAMEDATA\MODS\[/b][/u]

[b]#GUIDE#[/b]
A little help for installing mods manually:
[url=https://nomanssky.fandom.com/wiki/Mods#Installing_Mods]https://nomanssky.fandom.com/wiki/Mods#Installing_Mods[/url]
If you have installed the mod correctly and it still doesn't work,
try deleting [u][b]No Man's Sky\Binaries\SETTINGS\GCMODSETTINGS.MXML[/b][/u] file and restart the game

GLOBALS\GCBUILDABLESHIPGLOBALS.GLOBAL.EXML

name: Station Colour Fix
summary: Restores the procedural colours for abandoned and outlaw stations.
author: AyyMang
description: There's a mistake in the vanilla game that makes the textures used by the outlaw and abandoned stations appear turquoise instead of sampling their proper procedural colours. This mod simply fixes that bug, restoring the stations to their intended colourful states again.

name: Moar Realistic Weather
summary: Changes the temperature of some planet biomes.
author: FlorianB1 and Babscoole
description:
- no hot rain storms anymore
- no too hot dust storms
- no too cold dust nights
- not too cold on cold planets
- extreme temperatures on dead planets

This is an update for [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/nomanssky/mods/1964]More Realistic Weather[/url] to work with current NMS versions, with permission graciously granted.  Without [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/profile/FlorianB1]FlorianB1[/url] the mod would not exist.

Installation:
Extract archive contents to GAMEDATA\MODS

Files modified:
METADATA\SIMULATION\SOLARSYSTEM\WEATHER\BLUEWEATHER.MBIN
METADATA\SIMULATION\SOLARSYSTEM\WEATHER\CLEARCOLD.MBIN
METADATA\SIMULATION\SOLARSYSTEM\WEATHER\CLEARWEATHER.MBIN
METADATA\SIMULATION\SOLARSYSTEM\WEATHER\DUSTWEATHER.MBIN

For install and usage of AMUMSS to self update the mod or combine with other mods using the included Lua script please see [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/nomanssky/mods/2626]AMUMSS How To[/url]

name: zFlatbed Friction v6.23
summary: zFlatbed Friction greatly reduces the sliding and jostling of scrap in the Flatbed. Greatly reduces chance of exploding.
author: JMZ
description:
[color=#00ffff][size=5][b][u]zFlatbed Friction v6.23[/u][/b][/size][/color]

[color=#00ffff][size=3]zFlatbed [/size][size=3]Friction greatly reduces the sliding and jostling of scrap in the Flatbed. Greatly reduces chance of exploding.[/size][/color]

[color=#ff7700][size=3]After extensive play testing and fine tuning, this new version is amazing. I just completed a cross country trip on a nasty, bumpy planet and didn't lose a thing. Stunning actually.
[/size][/color]
[size=3]Driving cross country with a load of hazardous waste is difficult. The barrels in the flatbed want to roll around and bounce out. Excessive jostling can cause some to even explode. This mod increases the friction of the flatbed as well as the cargo. Rolling friction is greatly increased to reduce the risk of cargo becoming unstable due to damage.
Additionally, if a barrel falls off the truck, it rolls away more slowly and not as far.
You still need to drive carefully, but this mod should increase the success rate of your deliveries.[/size]

[color=#ff7700][size=3][u]Known Issues:[/u][/size][/color]
[size=3]This mod is of limited scope, changing only the physics entities for the flatbed and junk. It is distributed as an EXML patch. It should play nice with other mods that also employ proper EXML patch mods to this file.[/size]

[size=3]The zFlatbed Friction folder should be placed in your No Man’s Sky\GAMEDATA\MODS folder along with all of your other mods.[/size]

[color=#ffff00][size=3]This mod would not be possible without the use of the fabulous MBINcompiler.[/size][/color]

[color=#00ff00][size=3]I hope you find this mod useful and enjoy it.  If you do like this mod, I ask that you consider taking a moment to endorse it. Endorsements are the only real feedback that is reliable.[/size]

[size=3]Cheers, JMZ[/size][/color]
